wrapUp: Matchdays ten and eleven reshaped several group races. Neymar completed his first full training session as Raphinha was ruled out of Brazil's Scotland decider, Germany lost Nico Schlotterbeck for the remainder of the tournament despite qualifying from Group E, Japan's 4-0 win eliminated Tunisia on Hervé Renard's debut, the Netherlands thrashed Sweden 5-1 and Spain responded with four goals against Saudi Arabia as Lamine Yamal scored on his World Cup return. England welcomed Bukayo Saka back to full training while managing Declan Rice's hamstring complaint ahead of Ghana, and Christian Pulisic is expected to be fit for the United States' group finale but may be rested with Group D already won. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 22 June 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: Neymar completes first full training session ahead of Scotland
stories.summary: Neymar completed his first full training session with Brazil on 21 June after missing the draws with Morocco and the 3-0 win over Haiti with a grade-two calf strain sustained playing for Santos on 17 May. Carlo Ancelotti has said the 34-year-old will be included in the matchday squad for the Group C finale against Scotland on 24 June in Miami, potentially his first international appearance since October 2023. With Raphinha ruled out of that fixture, Neymar's return would give Brazil a proven outlet in attack before the knockout stage, though how many minutes he can manage after a month out remains to be seen.

stories.headline: Raphinha ruled out of Scotland match with hamstring injury
stories.summary: Raphinha will miss Brazil's Group C decider against Scotland on 24 June after scans confirmed a hamstring injury sustained in the first half of the 3-0 win over Haiti on 19 June. The CBF said the Barcelona forward will undergo intensive treatment with the aim of returning for a possible last-16 fixture in early July, but Lucas Paqueta acknowledged the squad are losing a key wide outlet for the Scotland match. Raphinha had started both group games and his absence opens a starting berth that Neymar is now expected to fill, though Brazil already face the tournament without Rodrygo, Estêvão and Wesley.

stories.headline: Saka returns to full training before England face Ghana
stories.summary: Bukayo Saka took part in normal training with England on 21 June after doing individual work on the previous day, providing Thomas Tuchel a fitness boost before the Group L meeting with Ghana in Boston on 23 June. The Arsenal winger is managing Achilles tendinitis that kept him on the bench for the 4-2 opening win over Croatia, and Tuchel has said he is unlikely to start until the Panama group finale. Saka's issue has not deteriorated since Arsenal's title run-in, but Tuchel's caution means Noni Madueke may continue on the right against Ghana as England chase a second win to tighten their grip on Group L.

stories.headline: Doku misses Belgium's goalless draw with Iran through illness
stories.summary: Jérémy Doku was unavailable for Belgium's 0-0 draw with Iran on 21 June in Los Angeles because of illness, depriving Rudi Garcia's side of their most direct wide outlet in a match they dominated without finding a breakthrough. Thibaut Courtois and Alireza Beiranvand produced several fine saves at either end before Nathan Ngoy was sent off in the 66th minute, yet Iran held on with ten men to leave both teams on two points in Group G. Doku's availability for Belgium's final group fixture will matter in a section where Egypt and New Zealand also remain in contention.

stories.headline: Schlotterbeck ruled out for remainder of World Cup
stories.summary: Nico Schlotterbeck has been ruled out of the rest of the World Cup after scans on 21 June confirmed a torn medial ligament in his left ankle, sustained during Germany's 2-1 win over Ivory Coast on 20 June in Toronto. The Borussia Dortmund centre-back played on until half-time before Antonio Rüdiger replaced him, and Julian Nagelsmann said Germany will miss his build-up play from the back as they head into the knockouts despite already topping Group E. Rüdiger is expected to partner Jonathan Tah in the last 32, with Waldemar Anton and Malick Thiaw as further options at centre-back.

stories.headline: Pulisic expected fit for Turkiye but Pochettino may rest captain
stories.summary: Christian Pulisic missed the 2-0 win over Australia on 19 June after training separately all week with a left calf problem sustained during the 4-1 opener against Paraguay, but reports suggest he should be fit for the Group D finale against Turkiye on 25 June. With the co-hosts already confirmed as group winners, Mauricio Pochettino may choose not to risk his captain in a dead rubber, prioritising full sharpness for the Round of 32 in Santa Clara on 1 July. Ricardo Pepi impressed as a starter in Seattle, giving Pochettino a credible alternative if Pulisic is held back for the knockouts.

stories.headline: Rice playing through hamstring neural pain ahead of Ghana
stories.summary: Declan Rice is expected to start against Ghana on 23 June despite being substituted as a precaution in the 72nd minute of England's 4-2 win over Croatia on 17 June, when he reported discomfort in his lower back and upper hamstring. Rice told ITV he has been managing neural pain in his hamstring since Christmas, though Thomas Tuchel described the Croatia change as protective rather than a reaction to a new serious injury. Jamie Carragher said England cannot afford to rest their midfield anchor with knockout qualification still to be secured, making Rice's availability central to Tuchel's balance in Group L.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Renard's Tunisia bow ends in 4-0 defeat and group exit
stories.summary: Hervé Renard's first match in charge of Tunisia ended in a 4-0 defeat to Japan on 21 June in Monterrey, eliminating the North Africans from Group F after just two matches. Ayase Ueda scored twice and Daichi Kamada opened the scoring inside four minutes in the 1,000th World Cup finals match, leaving Renard's side bottom of the group with zero points. The Frenchman was parachuted in after Sabri Lamouchi's sacking following a 5-1 opening loss to Sweden, but the mid-tournament reshuffle could not arrest a campaign already defined by heavy defeats.

stories.headline: Pepi starts as Pochettino rests Pulisic for Australia
stories.summary: Mauricio Pochettino handed Ricardo Pepi a first World Cup start against Australia on 19 June, leaving Christian Pulisic out of the matchday squad entirely as the co-hosts managed his calf injury. Pepi led the line in a high-pressing 2-0 win that secured knockout qualification, with Folarin Balogun's run forcing Cameron Burgess's own goal and Alex Freeman heading a second before half-time. Pochettino's decision to prioritise Pulisic's recovery over risking him in Seattle suggests the star winger is being targeted for the latter stages rather than the group finale against Türkiye.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: Almirón first player sent off for mouth-covering rule
stories.summary: Paraguay's Miguel Almirón became the first player dismissed under FIFA's new mouth-covering rule during the 1-0 win over Türkiye on 19 June, receiving a straight red in first-half stoppage time after obscuring his mouth while speaking to Mert Muldur. VAR reviewed the incident before referee Ivan Barton confirmed the dismissal, leaving Paraguay to defend a 64-second Matías Galarza goal with ten men for the entire second half. The IFAB-approved regulation is designed to curb abusive language and has already reshaped a knockout-race fixture in Group D.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: Japan's record win eliminates Tunisia in 1,000th World Cup match
stories.summary: Japan beat Tunisia 4-0 on 21 June in the 1,000th match in World Cup history, matching the Netherlands' statement result from earlier in the day to move level on four points at the top of Group F. Daichi Kamada's fourth-minute opener was the fastest goal by a Japanese player at a World Cup, Ayase Ueda scored twice and Junya Ito added a third as Hajime Moriyasu's side posted the largest victory by any nation in tournament history. Tunisia's elimination leaves Japan and the Netherlands fighting for top spot before the closing fixtures against each other.

stories.headline: Yamal and Oyarzabal fire Spain to first Group H win
stories.summary: Lamine Yamal scored on his first World Cup start and Mikel Oyarzabal added two more as Spain beat Saudi Arabia 4-0 on 21 June in Atlanta, rebounding from the shock goalless draw with Cape Verde in their opener. Luis de la Fuente's side were three goals ahead before the first hydration break, with Hassan Altambakti's own goal completing the scoring after half-time. Spain move top of Group H on four points and need only a point against Uruguay on 26 June to secure a Round of 32 berth, while Yamal's return eases concerns over the hamstring issue that had kept him out of the Cape Verde match.

stories.headline: Gakpo and Brobbey doubles send Netherlands top of Group F
stories.summary: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo each scored twice as the Netherlands beat Sweden 5-1 on 20 June in Houston to leapfrog their opponents into first place in Group F. Brobbey struck twice inside 17 minutes before Gakpo added a quickfire double after the break, with substitute Crysencio Summerville sealing a fifth in the 89th minute after Anthony Elanga had briefly reduced the arrears. The Oranje all but guaranteed a Round of 32 place with four points from two games, setting up a closing group decider against Japan with both sides level on points.

stories.headline: Saibari screamer leaves Scotland relying on best-third route
stories.summary: Ismael Saibari scored after 71 seconds as Morocco beat Scotland 1-0 on 19 June in Boston, registering the fastest goal of the tournament so far and becoming only the second African player to score in his first two World Cup appearances. Scotland improved after the break and had penalty appeals waved away, but John McGinn's claims that Morocco "got away with one" could not change a result that leaves Steve Clarke's side on three points in Group C. Scotland's most likely path to the Round of 32 now runs through the best-third-placed formula before a daunting closing fixture against Brazil.

stories.headline: Turkey crash out goalless after ten-man Paraguay hold on
stories.summary: Türkiye were eliminated from Group D on 19 June after a 1-0 defeat to ten-man Paraguay, completing a miserable campaign in which Vincenzo Montella's side took 62 shots across two matches without scoring. Matías Galarza's 64-second strike proved decisive before Miguel Almirón's red card, yet Türkiye dominated possession and created chances Arda Güler, Kenan Yildiz and Mert Muldur could not convert. Güler apologised to the nation after the loss, while Paraguay revive their knockout hopes ahead of a decider against Australia.
